description: AI models, agent frameworks, and dev tools relevant to Vigil system. Updated each RESEARCH scan.

tools.name: Anthropic (company regulatory status)
tools.status: MONITORING - April 2 is DOD 9th Circuit emergency stay deadline
tools.notes: April 2 is the deadline for DOD to seek a 9th Circuit emergency stay of the preliminary injunction blocking the federal agency ban on Claude. If no stay is filed today, Anthropic wins this round. OPERATE workstream should check for any 9th Circuit filing by end of day April 2. Vigil is not a federal contractor so no direct impact either way.
tools.vigil_impact: low direct risk. Server-side API not affected by federal contractor ban. But signals regulatory instability around Anthropic.

tools.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
tools.notes: Powers all Vigil workstreams except ADVISOR. Current production model. Stable.

tools.name: Claude Opus 4.6
tools.notes: Most capable Claude model currently in API. Used for ADVISOR. Strong planning and long-horizon tasks.

tools.name: Claude Mythos (Capybara)
tools.capabilities: Dramatically outperforms Opus 4.6 on coding, reasoning, cybersecurity. Currently cybersecurity defense use only.
tools.availability: No API yet. Piloting with select customers (cybersecurity defense only). Polymarket updated to approximately 25% odds of public access by April 30, 2026 (down from 45% for June 30). Consensus timing shifting toward Q3 2026. Possible IPO October 2026 may align with general API release late Q3/Q4.
tools.vigil_impact: critical - when available, should replace Opus for SECURE workstream. May upgrade ADVISOR.
tools.action: OPERATE to add monthly check: has Mythos API gone public? Check Anthropic release notes monthly.

tools.name: Claude Code
march_2026_features: /voice command - push-to-talk, 20 languages, hold spacebar to activate
Computer Use preview - point, click, navigate (Pro/Max plans)
64K default / 128K max output for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
--bare flag for scripted -p calls (skips hooks, LSP, plugin sync)
/effort command with 'ultrathink' keyword for max compute
known_bugs: Rate limit drain bug reported March 26 - suspected but unconfirmed
tools.vigil_impact: /loop could replace some OpenClaw cron jobs. Computer Use could enable visual dashboard verification. Agent Teams (CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS) now stable after March early-month patches (nested spawning bug and memory leak fixed). Vigil multi-session architecture could migrate to native Agent Teams, reducing OpenClaw orchestration overhead.
tools.agent_teams_status: production-stable as of March 2026. Requires v2.1.32+. Disabled by default. Enable via CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS in settings.json.

tools.name: GPT-5.4
tools.key_feature: Tool Search API for large toolset systems. Native computer use.
tools.vigil_impact: competitive intelligence only. We use Claude. But Tool Search architecture is worth monitoring for Vigil's tool routing design.

tools.name: xAI Grok 4.20
tools.key_feature: Completely new multi-agent architecture. Different from GPT and Claude agent frameworks.
tools.vigil_impact: monitor only. Multi-agent architecture worth studying for future Vigil design. Not replacing Claude.

tools.name: OpenClaw
tools.notes: OpenClaw gateway is the primary Claude session dispatcher for Vigil. Running stably since restart. Recent bug: cron list call blocked event loop (fixed 2026-03-28). cron jobs.json now read directly.
known_issues: FIXED 2026-03-28: openclaw cron list shell call was blocking Node.js event loop in dashboard server.js under circuit breaker conditions. Fixed by direct fs.readFileSync on jobs.json.

tools.name: Scrapling
tools.status: active - used by Vigil inbox pipeline
tools.notes: Used for URL scraping and content ingestion via inbox watcher. 5 files processed successfully today.
